summary: Updated pipeline_orchestrator.py to invoke new action skills (D1.1, D1.2, D1.3) instead of spawning /zo/ask workers with prompts

artifacts.description: Orchestrator v2 - invokes action skills and executes their orchestrator_instructions
Updated to document v2 architecture with action skills

capabilities_delivered: run command - Full automatic pipeline run for heartbeat agent
scan command - Returns Gmail queries for email discovery
scan --execute - Actually queries Gmail (placeholder for Zo tool calls)
process-one command - Process single email through appropriate skill
status command - Reports skill availability and config status
Parses orchestrator_instructions JSON from skill output
Prepares instructions for Zo to execute Airtable/Drive/Gmail operations

architecture_decisions.decision: Skills return orchestrator_instructions JSON rather than executing API calls directly
architecture_decisions.rationale: Keeps skills deterministic and testable. The orchestrator (running in Zo context) handles actual tool calls. This separation means skills can be tested with --dry-run without credentials.

architecture_decisions.decision: Orchestrator invokes skills via subprocess rather than importing
architecture_decisions.rationale: Skills may have different dependencies. Subprocess isolation prevents import conflicts and makes each skill independently runnable.

architecture_decisions.decision: Update handler uses a different pattern (direct API calls when key available)
architecture_decisions.rationale: D1.3 was built with a slightly different architecture. Works but should be standardized in future iteration.

test_results.status_command: PASS - All three skills detected, config loaded
test_results.scan_command: PASS - Returns proper Gmail query structure
test_results.process_update_dry_run: PARTIAL - Invokes skill but fails on missing AIRTABLE_API_KEY (expected)

D1.3_update_handler.status: integrated with note: uses direct API approach

heartbeat_integration.returns: JSON with Gmail queries and processing instructions
heartbeat_integration.requires: Heartbeat agent to execute Gmail queries and call process-one for each email

flags.description: D1.3 update handler uses direct API calls instead of orchestrator_instructions pattern
flags.recommendation: Consider standardizing in future iteration, but current state is functional

notes: The orchestrator is designed to be invoked by the heartbeat agent
For full end-to-end execution, the heartbeat agent needs to: (1) call run, (2) execute Gmail queries, (3) call process-one for each email, (4) execute orchestrator_instructions
Skills can be tested independently with --dry-run flag
Update handler needs AIRTABLE_API_KEY environment variable for non-dry-run execution
lessons_for_ledger: Action skills should output orchestrator_instructions JSON for Airtable/Drive/Gmail ops, letting orchestrator execute them in Zo context
Subprocess invocation provides good isolation for skills with different dependencies
When Wave 1 drops have slightly different patterns, note it but integrate anyway - standardize later
